[PDF_PAGE 21 | score=10] Chapter 1 Table 3: Shadow Mage Target having Throw Modifiers Lighting Conditions Modifier Bright daylight/continual light : +2 Weak daylight/dusk///^/)? none Twilight/moonlight/lantern light -1 Weak moonlight/torch light -2 Candlelight/starlight -3 Total darkness -4 essarily evil, most tend to be grim characters who are at home in the darkness. The school of shadow is built around the thesis that all shadows are actually connected in some mystical way in the Demiplane of Shadow; the shadow mage's repertoire of spells reflects this belief. Shadow mages must be keen-minded individuals; a character must have an Intelligence of 15 and a Wisdom of 16 (Intelli- gence/Reason and Wisdom/Will) in ord
[PDF_PAGE 124 | score=6] Spells in Combat Table 44: Spells with Knockdown Effects Knockdown Spell Abi-Dalzim's horrid wilting2 Bigby's forceful hand Bigby's crushing hand Bigby's grasping luind Bigby's clenched fist , blade barrier bone club call lightning cause serious wounds cause light zvounds cause critical wounds chain lightning chromatic orV claws of the umber hulk1 cone of cold delayed blast fireball dust devil explosive runes fire seeds (missile) fire trap fire storm fireball fist of stone 1 flame arrow flame strike Die dl2 dl2 dl2 dl2 d20 dlO d8 dl2 dlO d8 dl2 dl2 d6 dlO d8 d8 d6 dlO d8 d8 d8 d8 d8 dlO d8 1 From The Complete Wizard's Handbook 2 From Tome of Magic Spell gust of wind harm holy word ice knife'
[PDF_PAGE 148 | score=6] New Wizard Spells, 4th Level or a limited wish or wish. However, if the subject entertains thoughts of removing the lesser geas, it will protect itself by giving the subject a warning headache. If the subject per- sists, then the sickness will begin, Mordenkainen's Force Missiles (Invocation/Evocation, Force) Range: 30 yds. + 10 yds./level Duration: Instantaneous Area of Effect: 1-7 targets Subtlety: +3 Sensory: Medium visual, large audio Components: V, S Casting Time: 4 Saving Throw: Special Knockdown: dlO Critical: Medium (1 hit) impact This spell creates a brilliant globe of magical energy that streaks forth from the caster's hand to unerringly strike its target, much like a magic missile
[PDF_PAGE 152 | score=6] New Wizard Spells, 5th Level wizard's command. Each of the eyes is about the size of a small apple and can see 120 feet (normal vision only) in all directions. In order to report their findings, the eyes must return to the caster's hand to replay in the caster's mind everything they have seen during their existence. The eyes are subject to illusions, darkness, fog, and any other factors that would affect the wizard's ability to receive visual infor- mation about his surroundings. The eyes only see as a nor- mal human would—abilities and spell effects including infravision do not alter the eyes' vision. It only takes the eye one round to replay one hour of recorded images. The spell conjures 
[PDF_PAGE 188 | score=6] Priest Spells by Sphere Whirlwind (6th) Conjure Air Elemental (7th) Wind Walk (7th) Elemental, Earth (Major: druids; Minor: clerics) Strength of Stone (1st) Soften Earth and Stone (2nd) Meld into Stone (3rd) Stone Shape (3rd) Adamantite Mace (4th) Spike Stones (5th) Transmute Rock to Mud/Transmute Mud to Rock (5th) Stone Tell (6th) Animate Rock (7th) Antimineral Shell (7th) Conjure Earth Elemental (7th) Earthquake (7th) Transmute Metal to Wood (7th) Elemental, Fire (Major: druids) Firelight (1st) Log of Everburning (1st) Fire Trap (2nd) Flame Blade (2nd) Heat Metal/Chill Metal (2nd) Produce Flame (2nd) Flame Walk (3rd) Protection from Fire (3rd) Pyrotechnics (3rd) Produce Fire/Quench Fire (4
[PDF_PAGE 85 | score=5] Chapter 6 channelled the character doesn't actually spend his spell points until he actually casts a spell, and his battery of spells remains in his memory without vanishing. In other words, the warlock may make use of any of his memorized spells freely until he runs out of spell points. However, please note that the warlock may never cast more than nine spells of any one level in the course of a single day. However, all warlocks and witches have a serious limitation: Each time they spend spell points to cast a spell, they risk attracting the attention of a chaotic or evil power. The character has a percentage chance equal to the number of spell points expended, minus his charac- ter level, 
[PDF_PAGE 119 | score=4] Chapter 8 Table 39: &pell <§ubtlety Modifiers Modifier Condition +1 Casting time of 4 or higher +2 Casting time of 7 or higher +3 Casting time of one round or more +5 Casting time of one day or more +1 Includes verbal component +1 Includes somatic component +1 Per three material components included +1 Spell includes unusual action (singing, clapping, etc.) +2 Spell requires dramatic action (shatter- ing gem or breaking item, capering and dancing, shouting, etc.) +4 Spell requires very obvious action (a full religious ritual, drawing a circle on the ground with powder, etc.) +6 Character going out of his way to be noticed -2 Poor lighting -1 Background noise can mask sounds -2 Distracting act
[PDF_PAGE 120 | score=4] Spells in Combat reduced by as much as 50 to 75 percent. However, simple darkness may not conceal spells with brilliant or fiery effects; if anything, a fireball or lightning bolt is even more visible at nighttime than it is in daylight! Spells can usually be heard or smelled at half the range they can be seen and felt at one-tenth the visibility range. The roar of a fireball normally carries for 250 feet or so, even if the blast itself is out of sight around a corner or behind a hill. A deafened character who isn't looking at the blast can still feel the heat of the fireball from as far as 50 feet away. However, sound and smells may not propagate in a perfect circle from the spell's targeti
